round1: The Blackhawks' implosion in four games turned most brackets into confetti, leaving many of you questioning your life choices. While Glenda and Jaclyn managed to salvage some dignity, Jake, Jamie, and Nathan proved that picking the obvious (and wrong) teams is a fast track to the bottom.
round2: Round 2 is over, and it seems many of you forgot Anaheim was still in the league; KIERSTEN and NATHAN, however, clearly did their homework. For everyone who picked Washington in 7, enjoy your consolation prize of being wrong. At least a few of you managed to correctly pick Nashville, proving you're not entirely useless.
round3: Round 3 is done, and it seems Marc was the only one paying attention, perfectly nailing both series. While many correctly picked Nashville, predicting that Pens-Sens slugfest took some real clairvoyance – or just dumb luck for the five of you who scraped by with a single point this round.
round4: Pittsburgh took the Cup in six, making Glenda and Jaclyn look like prophets. Six of you picked Nashville to win; a special shout-out to Nickall, Robin, and Steph for guessing the wrong team AND the wrong series length.
overall: Well, that was a playoff pool for the ages, if by 'ages' you mean 'embarrassingly low scores across the board.' Glenda finally broke through for her first championship, sharing the glory with Jaclyn, who clearly remembered how to pick a winner after a seven-year hiatus from the top spot. But let's be real, the real story is Stephanie, who went from reigning champion last year with a whopping 63 points to a spectacular, soul-crushing zero this year, proving that gravity always wins. She wasn't alone in the basement, as Nickall added another last-place finish to his impressive collection of failures, and Robin decided to join the 'zero points club' for the first time, likely just to keep them company. Even multi-time champions like Marc and Nathan barely scraped by, demonstrating that even the 'experts' can completely whiff on a playoff bracket, while poor Jamie, Benedict, Jake, and Kiersten continue their championship droughts, apparently content with their perennial mediocrity.
